title: Buttery Fluffy Japanese Rice Curry
description: A quick, vegan-friendly Japanese curry that blends a bhuna‑style spice base with creamy, buttery rice, finished with a sprinkle of vegan Parmesan for a cheesy finish.
instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice until water runs clear, then combine with vegetable broth in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until rice is fluffy and liquid absorbed.
While the rice cooks, heat the neutral oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the skillet, sautéing for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the Japanese curry paste and cook for 1 minute, allowing the spices to bloom (bhuna technique).
Add the mixed vegetables to the skillet, stirring to coat them in the curry base.
Pour in the remaining vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce, tasting and adjusting seasoning as needed.

Remove the skillet from heat, fold in the vegan butter and stir until melted and the sauce is silky. Fluff the cooked rice with a fork, then combine with the curry, mixing gently to coat the grains. Sprinkle the nutritional yeast on top and serve immediately.
